---
orphan: true
---

# chord_sb2_et.yml

```yaml
# chord_sb2_et.yml: chord example with 25 subbands and 3 early triggers.
#
# This file format is used for on-disk dedispersion configs, and is one of
# three metadata files sent from pirate to the grouper.
#
# Note: params in this file are hardcoded into makefile_helper.py
# (when production_kernels are autogenerated), so any changes made
# here should be reflected there as well.

# Overrideable params: frequency channels, time sample length, number of beams.
# In a fully "production" context, these parameters get overridden by the real-time X-engine.
# In testing contexts (e.g. fake X-engine, test_dedisperser) these parameters are used "as is".

zone_freq_edges: [300, 350, 450, 600, 800, 1500]
zone_nfreq: [ 8192, 8192, 6144, 2048, 3584 ]   # U = 32,16,8,4,1 (28160 freq channels total)
time_sample_ms: 1.0
beams_per_gpu: 60

# Non-overrideable params start here.
dtype: float16
time_samples_per_chunk: 2048

# Frequency subbands are specified as an integer-valued array. This array
# corresponds to 25 subbands. For details/plots, see notes/tree_dedispersion.tex.
# Command line: pirate_frb make_subbands 300 1500 0.1 -r 4

frequency_subband_counts: [5,9,7,3,1]

# GPU configuration.
# FIXME: the 'max_gpu_clag' parameter is a hack to control GPU memory usage.
# If it's too large, then you'll run out of GPU memory. If it's too small, then
# dedispersion will be slow. Currently, it has to be tuned by hand in the config
# file -- I'll implement auto-tuning in the future.

max_gpu_clag: 5
beams_per_batch: 2
num_active_batches: 2

# Dedispersion trees. I'm currently using toplevel rank 16 in CHORD.
#
# Each "primary tree" searches a different DM range, ordered from low to high,
# and has early triggers (if needed) starting at ~60 sec dispersion delay. See
# the tex notes for detailed info.
#
# The 'max_width' param is the max peak-finding width (see tex note),
# in "time samples after downsampling by 2^(primary_tree_index)".
# To get ~32 ms, we set max_width=[32,16,8,4] in each primary tree.
#
# FIXME: the '*_downsampling' params are intended to control coarse-graining,
# but are currently in a state of incomplete implementation. For now, set
# dm_downsampling = time_downsampling = 0 to auto-select coarse-graining factors.
# The dedisperser will "choose them for you", and they need not be the same in
# all trees. The values chosen by the dedisperser will show up in the dedispersion
# plan yaml ('dm_downsampling' and 'time_downsampling', see example_dedispersion_plan.yml).
#
# If your downstream code depends on how they're chosen, then you should put asserts
# in your code, in case the values change in the future. This is something I hope
# to improve soon!

toplevel_tree_rank: 16

primary_trees:
  - {num_early_triggers: 0, max_width: 32, dm_downsampling: 0, time_downsampling: 0, wt_dm_downsampling: 64, wt_time_downsampling: 64}
  - {num_early_triggers: 1, max_width: 16, dm_downsampling: 0, time_downsampling: 0, wt_dm_downsampling: 64, wt_time_downsampling: 64}
  - {num_early_triggers: 2, max_width:  8, dm_downsampling: 0, time_downsampling: 0, wt_dm_downsampling: 64, wt_time_downsampling: 64}
  - {num_early_triggers: 3, max_width:  4, dm_downsampling: 0, time_downsampling: 0, wt_dm_downsampling: 64, wt_time_downsampling: 64}

# A WriteFiles request may extend into the future, by a bounded amount.
# In this config, early triggers may extend 448*1024 samples into the future.
# We add an extra ~5K samples, in case padding is desired.
# Note: the 'max_samples' bound only applies to WriteFiles requests.
# StartStreams requests can extend arbitrarily far into the future.

future_write_max_samples: 464000
```
